channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: What is known about a plane crash in Kazakhstan that killed 38 of 67 people on board
item.description: An Azerbaijani airliner has crashed in Kazakhstan after being diverted, killing 38 of 67 people on board.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: More than 10,000 migrants died this year trying to reach Spain by sea, aid group says
item.description: A Spanish migration rights group says more than 10,000 migrants died in 2024 trying to reach Spain by sea.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Russia arrests 4 suspects accused of plotting to kill top military officers on Ukraine's orders
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russia's top security agency said Thursday that it has arrested several suspects accused of involvement in an alleged Ukrainian plot to assassinate senior military officers, an announcement that follows the killing of a top Russian general last week.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Azerbaijan observes day of mourning for air crash victims as speculation mount about its cause
item.description: Azerbaijan is observing a nationwide day of mourning for the victims of the air crash that killed 38 people and left all 29 survivors injured as speculation mounted about a possible cause of the crash that remained unknown.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Chinese automaker BYD slams reports of poor conditions at a factory site in Brazil
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A spokesperson for Chinese automaker BYD has objected to reports about poor conditions at a construction site in Brazil where it is building a factory, saying the allegations were aimed at “smearing” China and Chinese brands.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli strike kills 5 Palestinian journalists in Gaza, officials say
item.description: Gaza's Health Ministry says an Israeli strike killed five Palestinian journalists outside a hospital overnight.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Former Presidential candidate in Taiwan, Ko Wen-je, indicted for corruption
item.description: Prosecutors in Taiwan indicted former presidential candidate and Taiwan People’s Party founder Ko Wen-je on corruption charges, accusing him of taking bribes during his time as mayor of Taiwan’s capital.
item.dc:creator: Huizhong Wu And Johnson Lai, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump announces ambassador to Panama amid fight over canal
item.description: President-elect Trump on Wednesday announced Miami-Dade County Commissioner Kevin Marino Cabrera as his pick for U.S. ambassador to Panama. The announcement comes as Trump is sparking a feud with the U.S. ally over control of the Panama Canal.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: A baby froze to death overnight in Gaza as Israel and Hamas trade accusations of ceasefire delays
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — A baby girl froze to death overnight in Gaza, while Israel and Hamas accused each other of complicating ceasefire efforts that could wind down the 14-month war.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK, TIA GOLDENBERG and FATMA KHALED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Taliban claims Pakistani airstrikes killed 46
item.description: A Taliban government official said the airstrikes Pakistan launched on Afghanistan Tuesday killed 46 people, including women and children, according to The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Israel's Benjamin Netanyahu wishes a merry Christmas to Christians around the world
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu wished Christians worldwide a Merry Christmas and thanked the Christian community for its support.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Turkey's soaring costs are creating a 'lost generation' of kids forced to help their families get by
item.description: ISTANBUL (AP) — In a dim one-room apartment in one of Istanbul’s poorest neighborhoods, 11-year-old Atakan Sahin curls up on a threadbare sofa with his siblings to watch TV while their mother stirs a pot of pasta. The simple meal is all the family of six can look forward to most evenings.
item.dc:creator: ROBERT BADENDIECK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cities worldwide hold subdued Christmas Eve celebrations amid conflicts
item.description: BETHLEHEM, West Bank (AP) — Bethlehem marked another somber Christmas Eve on Tuesday in the traditional birthplace of Jesus under the shadow of war in Gaza. The cheer that typically descends on the West Bank during Christmas week were nowhere to be found.
item.dc:creator: JALAL BWAITEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russian court sentences US citizen to 15 years in jail for espionage
item.description: A Russian court has sentenced a U.S. citizen to 15 years in jail for espionage on Tuesday, according to Russian state media outlets. Eugene Spector, who was born in Russia but later moved to the U.S. and got his U.S. citizenship, was already serving a 3 1/2-year sentence for bribery.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: White House briefs Trump administration on nuclear threats
item.description: On Christmas Eve, tensions in the Holy Land persist as fears over nuclear weapon production in Iran and Pakistan amid the ongoing Israel war.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: US military carries out airstrike in Syria, killing 2 ISIS operatives
item.description: The U.S. military conducted an airstrike on Monday in Syria, where they killed a pair of ISIS operatives, wounded another and destroyed a truckload of weapons.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Police officer dressed as the 'Grinch' steals Christmas spirit during drug bust
item.description: The Peruvian National Police canceled Christmas early for three alleged drug traffickers during a holiday-themed takedown that was captured on camera.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran recruiting children to attack Israeli targets across Europe amid setbacks: report
item.description: Iran has been hiring young people in Europe to attack Israeli and Jewish targets in countries across the continent, according to media reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NATO leaders predict era of 2% defense spending 'probably history' as Trump reportedly floats higher target
item.description: European leaders are making clear ahead of the Trump presidency that they plan to spend more on defense as Russia remains a top threat and as security concerns in the Middle East and North Africa escalate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump could face renewed ISIS threat in Syria as Turkey goes after US ally
item.description: Concerns are mounting that President-elect Trump could face an ISIS resurgence in Syria as Turkey-backed forces hit U.S.-allied SDF troops in a move that could divide the coalitions abilities to fight ISIS.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Christmas in Puerto Rico is a 45-day celebration with caroling, festive decorations, family feasts and more
item.description: Christmas in Puerto Rico is spent with loved ones singing carols, sitting around the table for a meal and exchanging gifts. There celebrations last from Thanksgiving to mid-January.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: German authorities received tipoffs last year about the suspect in Christmas market attack
item.description: MAGDEBURG, Germany (AP) — German authorities said they received tipoffs last year about the suspect in a car attack at a Christmas market in Magdeburg as more details emerged on Sunday about the five people killed.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL PROBST and VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Santa Claus is en route in his flight across the world to deliver gifts
item.description: When Saint Nick starts his journey on Dec. 24, families can track Santa's sleigh using the NORAD Santa tracker.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US military conducts successful airstrikes on Houthi rebel forces in Yemen
item.description: The U.S. military on Saturday said it conducted precision airstrikes on a missile storage site and a command-and-control center in Yemen against Houthi rebels.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: More than 30 dead in Brazil bus and truck collision
item.description: Over 30 people were killed after a packed bus collided with a truck in Brazil; the other passengers were transported to a local hospital and remain in critical condition.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: In a calendar rarity, Hanukkah starts this year on Christmas Day
item.description: Hanukkah, Judaism’s eight-day Festival of Lights, begins this year on Christmas Day, which has only happened four times since 1900. For some rabbis, the intersection of the two religious holidays provides an auspicious occasion for interfaith engagement.
item.dc:creator: DAVID CRARY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: At least 2 dead and 60 hurt after a car drives into a German Christmas market in a suspected attack
item.description: MAGDEBURG, Germany (AP) — A car plowed into a busy outdoor Christmas market in the eastern German city of Magdeburg on Friday, killing at least two people and injuring at least 60 others in what authorities called a deliberate attack.
item.dc:creator: EBRAHIM NOROOZI, CHRIS STERN and GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Malaysia agrees to resume 'no find, no fee' hunt for flight MH370, 10 years after plane disappeared
item.description: Over 10 years after flight MH370 is believed to have crashed in the Indian Ocean, Malaysia's government has agreed to accept a "no find, no fee" proposal from a U.S. company to resume the hunt.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden missing in action as Turkey inches closer to full-blown war against US-allied Kurds in Syria
item.description: Amid the end of the nearly 14 years of civil war in Syria, Turkish strongman Recep Tayyip Erdoğan is set to rekindle a new war in northern Syria against America’s main ally, the Syrian Kurds.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
